title: Fix 3 Correctness Issues: Response Contract, Subscription Leak, Reconnect Loop
description: Fix ISS-20260621-001 (WorkflowCommander response parsing), ISS-20260621-002 (+page.svelte subscription leak), and ISS-20260621-003 (stores/index.ts infinite reconnect loop).
action:
Fix 1 (ISS-001): In src/lib/client/components/WorkflowCommander.svelte line 23, change `.then((data: WorkflowMeta[]) => { workflows = data; loading = false; })` to `.then((data: { workflows: WorkflowMeta[] }) => { workflows = data.workflows; loading = false; })`. Also fix the retry handler at line 92 to match.

Fix 2 (ISS-002): In src/routes/+page.svelte line 14-17, replace the leaky $effect that subscribes to connectionState. Use Svelte's auto-subscription syntax: replace the entire $effect block with a simple `$effect(() => { connected = $connectionState.connected; });` which does not create manual subscriptions. Alternatively, remove the intermediate `connected` variable and use `$connectionState.connected` directly in the template.

Fix 3 (ISS-003): In src/lib/client/stores/index.ts lines 88-92, remove the `connectionState.subscribe()` that calls `wsClient.connect()`. Instead, add a one-time `wsClient.connect()` call immediately after the WSClient instantiation (around line 7). WSClient already handles reconnection internally via `scheduleReconnect()`.

criteria: stores/index.ts does NOT contain `connectionState.subscribe(($state) => { if (!$state.connected) { wsClient.connect()` (no reconnect loop)
stores/index.ts contains `wsClient.connect()` as a top-level call (one-time init)
npx vitest run exits 0 (all existing tests pass)

implementation.detail: Line 23: change `.then((data: WorkflowMeta[]) => { workflows = data; loading = false; })` to `.then((data: { workflows: WorkflowMeta[] }) => { workflows = data.workflows; loading = false; })`. Line 92 retry handler: same response shape fix.

Remove the $effect block at lines 14-17 that creates unmanaged subscriptions. Replace with a reactive derived: either use `let connected = $derived($connectionState.connected)` or `$effect(() => { connected = $connectionState.connected; })` which accesses the auto-subscription directly without creating manual `.subscribe()` calls.

Remove lines 88-92 (the connectionState.subscribe auto-connect). Add `wsClient.connect()` call at line 8 (immediately after WSClient instantiation): `export const wsClient = new WSClient('ws://localhost:3001'); if (typeof window !== 'undefined') { wsClient.connect(); }`
